name: BlueSnap Global Payment Orchestration API
what_it_does: BlueSnap global payment orchestration REST API for B2B SaaS, e-commerce, and marketplace businesses to accept payments in 100+ currencies, manage subscriptions, split marketplace payments, and optimize authorization rates with built-in fraud protection. Enables AI agents to manage global payment transaction processing for multi-currency commerce automation, handle subscription and recurring billing management for SaaS billing automation, access marketplace payment splitting and vendor management for platform payment automation, retrieve fraud detection and chargeback management for payment risk automation, manage payment method tokenization and vault for secure customer payment automation, handle tax calculation and reporting for compliance automation, access authorization rate optimization for payment performance automation, retrieve transaction reporting and financial reconciliation for accounting automation, manage alternative payment method integration for global payment method automation, and integrate BlueSnap with Salesforce, NetSuite, HubSpot, and business platforms for end-to-end global payment automation.
use_cases: Processing global B2B payments from multi-currency commerce agents
Managing SaaS subscription billing from recurring revenue automation agents
Splitting marketplace payments from platform payment agents
Optimizing authorization rates from payment performance agents
not_for: Simple US-only payment processing (use Square or Authorize.net for US-simple)
Consumer-facing marketplace payments at Stripe scale (use Stripe Connect for PLG marketplaces)
Crypto and emerging payment rails (use Coinbase Commerce or BitPay)
best_when: A B2B SaaS or global e-commerce company wanting AI agents to automate multi-currency payment processing, subscription billing, marketplace payment splitting, and authorization rate optimization through BlueSnap's all-in-one payment orchestration platform.
avoid_when: VENDOR-MANAGED PAYOUTS REQUIRE ONBOARDING: BlueSnap marketplace vendor payouts require vendor account onboarding (KYC/AML); automated marketplace payout workflow must coordinate vendor onboarding before payout enablement; automated payout to unverified vendor account creates payout failure or compliance hold. TAX CALCULATION SERVICE REGIONAL VARIATION: BlueSnap's built-in tax calculation covers major jurisdictions but may not handle all local tax rules; automated global tax compliance using BlueSnap tax must verify jurisdiction coverage; automated tax calculation without jurisdiction verification creates incorrect tax collection in uncovered regions. AUTH RATE OPTIMIZATION REQUIRES ROUTING RULE CONFIGURATION: BlueSnap's intelligent payment routing and auth rate optimization requires proper acquiring bank configuration; automated payment routing depends on BlueSnap account setup quality; automated payment optimization without configured routing rules defaults to single-path processing without optimization benefits.

auth.notes: BlueSnap uses API key (username/password Basic Auth) for authentication. REST API with JSON/XML. Waltham, Massachusetts HQ. Founded 2001 (formerly Plimus). Backed by Great Hill Partners, Temasek ($225M+ raised). Products: Global payment processing, subscription billing, marketplace payouts, fraud protection, tax compliance. Integrations: Salesforce, NetSuite, HubSpot, Zuora. PCI DSS Level 1. GDPR. Serves B2B SaaS and e-commerce globally. Competes with Adyen, Checkout.com, and PayU for global payments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise sales process
pricing.paid_tiers: Custom pricing based on volume; typically interchange-plus or blended rates; monthly minimum fees
pricing.notes: Waltham MA. Great Hill Partners backed. Custom volume-based pricing. Annual contract typical.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; transaction and subscription queries throttled; webhooks for payment, subscription, and chargeback events

known_agent_gotchas: XML vs JSON SUPPORT VARIES BY ENDPOINT: BlueSnap API supports both XML and JSON but some endpoints have format limitations; automated integration should default to JSON; automated XML-based integration may encounter JSON-only endpoints in newer API versions

SHOPPER ID vs VAULTED SHOPPER FOR RECURRING BILLING: BlueSnap uses 'vaulted shopper' object for stored payment methods; automated recurring billing must create vaulted shopper with stored card before subscription setup; automated subscription creation without vaulted shopper requires card data on every transaction

MARKETPLACE VENDOR SPLIT CALCULATION RESPONSIBILITY: BlueSnap marketplace payouts require explicit split calculation in API request; automated marketplace payment routing must calculate platform fee vs vendor payout split; automated split without fee calculation creates full vendor payout without platform revenue capture

CHARGEBACK RESPONSE WINDOW FOR AUTOMATED DISPUTE MANAGEMENT: BlueSnap chargeback responses have deadline-based windows (typically 7-20 days by card network); automated chargeback management must monitor dispute age; automated dispute response after deadline creates lost dispute regardless of evidence quality

SUBSCRIPTION PLAN vs CONTRACT DISTINCTION: BlueSnap subscriptions can be plan-based (catalog plan) or contract-based (custom terms); automated subscription management must use consistent model; automated plan-based subscription for enterprise contracts with custom pricing creates plan mismatch
agent_readiness.security_notes: Global payments. PCI-DSS Level 1. GDPR. API key. US/EU. Payment and subscription data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Global payments. PCI-DSS Level 1. GDPR. API key. US/EU. Payment data.
